_comment: Hand-computed ground truth. Every entry derived in data/sample/README.md. The Java IllustrativeDatasetGroundTruthTest verifies the loader sees the same numbers; once the miner is implemented, IllustrativeMinerGroundTruthTest will verify the algorithm produces these pattern sets exactly.

patterns.name: path A-B-C
patterns._highlight: AT sigma_min=6, tau_w=0.5: AVG declares P2 frequent (6/6), MIN rejects (5/6). This is the running example for the MIN-vs-AVG divergence.

patterns.name: triangle A-B-C
patterns._note: Second MIN-vs-AVG divergence. At tau_w=0.5: under MIN both G3 (W_min=0.10) and G4 (W_min=0.40) fail the floor, so sigma_MIN=0. Under AVG both pass (G3 avg=0.55, G4 avg=0.617), so sigma_AVG=2. At sigma_min=2 the two methods disagree by exactly {P4}.

patterns.name: star B at center, leaves A,C,D

expected_frequent_sets._highlight: Measure-divergence case study. The 1-edge B-C clears under both aggregators because every graph has a B-C edge with weight >= 0.5. The DIVERGENCE happens at the 2-edge layer: AVG accepts path A-B-C (P2) because G3 avg=0.525 >= 0.5 even though G3's A-B edge is severely weak (0.10); MIN correctly rejects P2 because W_min(P2, G3)=0.10 < 0.5. Symmetric difference = {P2}.
